2604.24114.title: IRIS: Interleaved Reinforcement with Incremental Staged Curriculum for Cross-Lingual Mathematical Reasoning
2604.24114.paper_summary: Curriculum learning helps language models tackle complex reasoning by gradually increasing task difficulty. However, it often fails to generate consistent step-by-step reasoning, especially in multilingual and low-resource settings where cross-lingual transfer from English to Indian languages remains limited. We propose IRIS: Interleaved Reinforcement with Incremental Staged Curriculum, a two-axis framework that combines Supervised Fine-Tuning on progressively harder problems (vertical axis) with Reverse Curriculum Reinforcement Learning to reduce reliance on step-by-step guidance (horizontal axis). We design a composite reward combining correctness, step-wise alignment, continuity, and numeric incentives, optimized via Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO). We release CL-Math, a dataset of 29k problems with step-level annotations in English, Hindi, and Marathi. Across standard benchmarks and curated multilingual test sets, IRIS consistently improves performance, with strong results on math reasoning tasks and substantial gains in low-resource and bilingual settings, alongside modest improvements in high-resource languages.
2604.24114.paper_summary_zh: 課程學習幫助語言模型通過逐漸增加任務難度來應對複雜的推理。然而，它經常無法生成一致的逐步推理，特別是在多語言和資源匱乏的環境中，從英語到印度語言的跨語言轉移仍然有限。我們提出了 IRIS：交錯增強與漸進階段課程，一個結合了在逐漸困難的問題上進行監督微調（垂直軸）和反向課程增強學習以減少對逐步指導依賴（水平軸）的雙軸框架。我們設計了一個複合獎勵，結合了正確性、逐步對齊、連續性和數值激勵，通過群體相對政策優化（GRPO）進行優化。我們發布了 CL-Math，一個包含 29,000 個問題的數據集，並提供英語、印地語和馬拉地語的逐步標註。在標準基準和精心策劃的多語言測試集上，IRIS 一貫提高性能，在數學推理任務上表現強勁，並在資源匱乏和雙語環境中取得顯著增長，同時在高資源語言中也有適度的改善。
2604.24114.author: Navya Gupta et.al.
2604.24114.authors: Navya Gupta, Rishitej Reddy Vyalla, Avinash Anand, Chhavi Kirtani, Erik Cambria, Zhengchen Zhang, Zhengkui Wang, Timothy Liu, Aik Beng Ng, Simon See, Rajiv Ratn Shah
